seo.title: CrewAI Guardrails for Education
seo.description: Compliance guide for CrewAI agents in education. Meet FERPA, COPPA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Education teams using CrewAI face unique risks when deploying multi-agent systems for tasks like personalized tutoring or administrative automation, as student data and content generation must comply with stringent privacy laws like FERPA and COPPA. Without proper guardrails, agent delegation loops or hallucinated outputs can expose sensitive student information or generate inappropriate content. Vex provides essential compliance checks at each task step to prevent data leaks, ensure age-appropriate outputs, and maintain consistent quality across agent roles.

complianceRequirements.description: Protects student education records from unauthorized disclosure, requiring parental consent for minors and strict data handling.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Scans agent inputs/outputs for PII like student IDs or grades, blocking tasks that risk exposure and logging consents.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates verifiable parental consent before collecting personal information from children under 13.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Detects child-related data in CrewAI tasks and enforces consent checks before agent processing or tool calls.

complianceRequirements.description: Vary by state but generally require encryption, access controls, and breach notifications for student data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Validates data flows between CrewAI agents for encryption compliance and flags unauthorized sharing.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires educational content and tools to be accessible to users with disabilities.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Checks generated outputs for WCAG compliance, ensuring readable text, alt text, and semantic structure.

industryRisks.name: Student Data Exposure
industryRisks.description: Agents hallucinate or loop-delegate containing student PII, leading to unencrypted sharing.
industryRisks.example: Tutor agent shares unredacted grades in task handoff to admin agent.

industryRisks.name: Inappropriate Content Generation
industryRisks.description: Task drift causes agents to produce age-inappropriate or biased educational materials.
industryRisks.example: Content agent generates mature themes for K-8 math tutoring due to objective reinterpretation.

industryRisks.name: FERPA Violation via Tool Calls
industryRisks.description: Hallucinated tool outputs include protected data without consent verification.
industryRisks.example: Grading agent calls external API with student names lacking parental consent.

industryRisks.name: Inaccessible Outputs
industryRisks.description: Crew outputs fail Section 508 due to inconsistent formatting across roles.
industryRisks.example: Summary agent produces image-heavy reports without alt text.

implementation.title: PII Detection in Agent Tasks
implementation.description: Integrate Vex callback to scan for student data before task execution.

implementation.title: Content Safety and Age-Appropriateness Check
implementation.description: Block harmful or biased content in educational outputs using Vex policies.
implementation.code:
from crewai import Crew
from vex_sdk.policies import EducationContentPolicy

policy = EducationContentPolicy(age_group='K-12', bias_check=True)

class SafeCrew(Crew):
    def _execute_task(self, task):
        pre_check = vex.evaluate(policy, task.description)
        if not pre_check:
            return 'Blocked: Inappropriate content'
        result = super()._execute_task(task)
        post_check = vex.evaluate(policy, result)
        return result if post_check else 'Blocked: Output violation'

crew = SafeCrew(agents=[tutor, reviewer], tasks=[lesson_task])

implementation.title: Prevent Delegation Loops with Compliance
implementation.description: Monitor agent handoffs for loops and compliance drift.

caseStudyScenario.heading: CrewAI in Education: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized school district deploys CrewAI to create personalized tutoring crews for 5th-grade math. The crew consists of a Planner Agent that interprets student performance data, a Tutor Agent that generates lessons, and a Reviewer Agent that ensures alignment with curriculum standards. Student data including names, grades, and learning gaps flows between agents during daily sessions for 2,000 students. Without guardrails, the Planner hallucinates missing data points, the Tutor drifts into advanced topics inappropriate for 10-year-olds, and delegation loops occur when the Reviewer repeatedly sends tasks back, risking FERPA violations through unmonitored PII sharing.

The district initially faces issues when the Tutor Agent generates a lesson referencing pop culture unsuitable for elementary students, and a tool call to an external math API accidentally includes unredacted student IDs.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex as task callbacks, all agent outputs are scanned for PII, content appropriateness, and accessibility. Delegation loops are detected and blocked after 3 cycles, preventing infinite task spinning. The Tutor Agent's outputs are flagged and corrected for age-appropriateness, while PII is automatically redacted before tool calls. The district achieves 100% FERPA compliance, reduces inappropriate content incidents to zero, and maintains accreditation during an audit. Vex logs provided evidence of proactive compliance measures.

cta.heading: Comply with Education Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex with CrewAI today to protect student data and ensure compliant AI tutoring.
